titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Wilfrid Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender, 17 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Wilfrid Bliss Spender

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter is part of a series of correspondence from Sir Wilfrid Bliss Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender. The letters date mainly from 1915 to 1918, when Sir Wilfrid was serving in France. The letters concern politics, the Irish question, personal life, and Wilfrid's experience of the Great War.In this letter Spender is writing to his wife, Lady Lillian Spender, née Dean, (1880-1968). Sir Wilfrid Spender (1876 - 1960) was a member of the British Army until 1913 when controversy surrounding his signature of the Ulster Solemn League and Covenant resulted in his resignation. Spender was called up for military service in 1914 and from 1915 served in the 36th (Ulster) Division. Throughout the war, Spender remained in contact with the Ulster Unionist leadership, and consistently opposed Home Rule and the six-county solution.In this letter, Spender suggests that Lillian send him chocolate and advises her on financial matters.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
June 17 Quite forgot the 16th
Really not an important
day at all as the whole
matter was settled long
before it & tho B.W. knew
this quite well no matter
what the pr may say.
She had no more idea
of saying no really than
she has of refusing chocolate. A chocolate wolf would be
quite irresistible. What I am not quite
clear about is what interest
16.6.15 you got on your bond mean-
while. Does it only cost
Â£900 or is it not Â£1000 in
which case your will want to
put in another Â£424 Your bw WB Spender
